content.problemStatement:
"I don't have any fancy quotes." - vijju123

Chef was reading some quotes by great people. Now, he is interested in classifying all the fancy quotes he knows. He thinks that all fancy quotes which contain the word "not" are Real Fancy; quotes that do not contain it are regularly fancy.

You are given some quotes. For each quote, you need to tell Chef if it is Real Fancy or just regularly fancy.
constraints: each character of S is either a lowercase English letter or a space
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single string S denoting a quote.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string "Real Fancy" or "regularly fancy" (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.input:
2
i do not have any fancy quotes
when nothing goes right go left
sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, the string contains the word "not", so the output is "Real Fancy". In the second test case, the string does not contain the word "not", so the output is "regularly fancy".
The string contains the word "not", so the output is "Real Fancy".
The string does not contain the word "not", so the output is "regularly fancy".

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.input:
1
i do not have any fancy quotes

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.input:
1
when nothing goes right go left

solution.approach: The approach to solve this problem is to iterate over each test case and check if the string contains the word "not". If it does, print "Real Fancy", otherwise print "regularly fancy". This can be achieved by using the 'includes' or 'indexOf' method in JavaScript, the 'find' method in C++, the 'indexOf' method in Java, the 'in' operator in Python, or the 'strstr' function in C.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * |S|) — where T is the number of test cases and |S| is the maximum length of a string. This is because for each test case, we are potentially scanning the entire string to check for the word "not".
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant because we are not using any data structures that scale with the input size.
